title: hibbertia aurea var. obtusa
intro: Hibbertia aurea var. obtusa, also known as the Yellow Pea flower or Dune Guinea flower, is a small, yellow flowering plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its leaves and flowers displaying anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide relief from fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, while also offering antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Hibbertia aurea var. obtusa can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a treatment.
